target: Create new WIN-070 covering the dome home-page Forensic Zoom chart forensics (cut from WIN-012 compression EXP-401, ~270 words). Structurally distinct from WIN-012 (κ undefined under own nulls) and WIN-058b (κ not derived / r=0.992 unremarkable) — this attack targets the chart presentation itself as a fabricated forensic exhibit.
scope_summary: New wins.json entry (id 070) using verdict 'Refuted by Data'. Recovers and updates the 270-word Exhibit-A graph forensics argument that was cut from WIN-012 detail_evidence in EXP-401 (commit 66c9a741). Pinned dome SHA c8eaeb1b (2026-05-17) for verification provenance. Kernel-of-truth: BOU magnetometer top-panel data IS real INTERMAGNET data and the dome correctly identifies the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm context; the failure is the bottom-panel L1A/µGal unit clash, the non-existent 'Induction Residual' product name, the GRACE orbital impossibility for co-temporal comparison, the absence of any GRACE/AI processing code in the dome repo, and the static-HTML provenance of the r and AI numbers. Cross-references WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted) and WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated) as parallel attacks on the same κ number from different production stages.
kernel_of_truth: Real BOU magnetometer data on top. The dome correctly identifies the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm peak (Dst = −383 nT) and the BOU station. The ±20.6 to +23.4 nT ΔH excursion is consistent with INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data for that event. So the dome is not making the entire figure up — it is pulling an authentic public product for the magnetic-side trace and then synthesizing an impossible bottom-panel quantity to pair with it. The kernel locates the failure precisely: the chart is not wholesale fabrication, it is fabricated atop a real anchor — which is what makes it more dangerous as a public-facing forensic exhibit.

proposed_win_entry.claim: Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis
proposed_win_entry.finding: Top-panel BOU magnetometer trace is real INTERMAGNET data; bottom panel labels an impossible product (GRACE L1A is pre-inversion telemetry, not µGal). No GRACE parser, no µGal computation, no AI estimator in repo; r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are static HTML.
proposed_win_entry.detail_claim: The dome's home page (john09289.github.io/predictions/, accessed 2026-05-17, dome SHA c8eaeb1b) presents a "Forensic Zoom" chart titled "Evidence Exhibit A: The 2003 Bedrock Audit." The chart purports to show 1-second-resolution data from the October 30, 2003 G5 Halloween storm (Dst = −383 nT, 00:55–01:22 UTC) covering two panels: (top) Boulder (BOU) magnetometer ΔH variance from INTERMAGNET, and (bottom) "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)" claimed as superconducting-gravimeter and GRACE-satellite-derived data showing a phase-locked downward gravitational pulse correlated with the magnetic excursion. The dome reports r = 0.992 (Raw L1A residuals) and an Asymmetry Index AI = 1.152, stating these statistics prove the 1.67 nT/µGal cavity transduction constant κ. The home-page Exhibit B then claims that a "Level-1B Administrative Mask" (a low-pass Butterworth filter applied by "standard geophysical agencies") erases the induction spikes from public data, leaving the "raw L1A binaries" — which the dome claims to have unmasked — as the only place the firmament resonance survives.
proposed_win_entry.detail_evidence:
The kernel: the top panel checks out, but everything below the magnetometer trace fails. Independent verification using INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive data confirms the BOU magnetometer top panel is plausible: the ±20.6 to +23.4 nT ΔH excursion is consistent with the October 30, 2003 G5 Halloween storm at Boulder during the published Dst = −383 nT recovery (one of the most extreme geomagnetic events ever recorded). The dome correctly identifies the date, the storm magnitude, and the BOU station. That is the kernel. Everything below the magnetometer trace fails.

(1) The label "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)" is internally impossible. GRACE Level-1A products are pre-inversion instrument data: accelerometer outputs in m/s² (ACC1A), K-band range-rates in m/s (KBR1A), and star-camera quaternions (SCA1A) — see JPL PO.DAAC conventions and JPL D-22027 (L1A product specification). The unit µGal (= 10−8 m/s²) is a post-processing unit applied to retrieved time-variable gravity at surface points, not to raw L1A telemetry. A y-axis label that reads both "Raw L1A" and "µGal" simultaneously is labeling raw instrument telemetry with a derived-product unit — the same physical quantity is being assigned two incompatible production stages in one phrase.

(2) "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual" is not a GRACE data product name. Actual L1A product naming follows the convention ACC1A / KBR1A / SCA1A. There is no GRACE pipeline stage called "Induction Residual" at any level (L0, L1A, L1B, L2, or L3) — GRACE measures inter-satellite range-rates and accelerations, from which time-variable gravity is later inverted. "Induction" belongs to electromagnetic physics, not to the GRACE accelerometer/KBR pipeline. The phrase "Raw L1A Induction Residual" is a category error: it splices a magnetic-field concept onto a gravity-mission product name.

(3) GRACE orbital mechanics rule out co-temporal comparison with a fixed Boulder station. GRACE flew in a near-polar orbit at ~500 km altitude at ~7.5 km/s. In the 27-minute window the dome chart depicts (00:55–01:22 UTC, Oct 30 2003), GRACE traversed ~12,150 km of ground track. A "phase-locked" co-temporal alignment with a fixed point at BOU (40.14°N, 105.24°W) is physically possible for at most ~2 minutes per pass when GRACE is overhead — not for a continuous 27-minute trace. Whatever the chart's bottom panel shows, it cannot be GRACE measurements co-located with BOU for the full window.

(4) The dome repository contains zero GRACE processing code. A repo-wide audit at dome SHA c8eaeb1b (May 17, 2026) finds no GRACE Level-1A binary parser, no accelerometer calibration pipeline, no K-band range-rate ingest, no CRN1B or CLK1B reader, and no µGal residual computation. Two files mention GRACE substantively: docs/Untitled-1.py (an exploratory NASA CMR URL-finder that never executes any L1A processing) and scripts/download_l1a_manual.py (a generic cookie-authenticated downloader with no GRACE-specific logic). The chart's claimed statistics — r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 — appear in docs/index.html as static HTML strings and in docs/data/storm_master.csv as unrelated column values; no script in the repository computes either number from any GRACE or BOU data. This is the same pattern the review documents for the dome's headline 95.2% accuracy figure: computed nowhere, displayed everywhere.

(5) The "Asymmetry Index" definition is also missing from the code. The dome page states "AI = 1.152" and explains "an AI of 1.152 proves the jitter is 15% more biased toward a downward push." But the repository contains no AI formula — no documented binning, no reference baseline, no asymmetry estimator. A dome defender can substitute any of several definitions (upward/downward bin ratio, skewness, Bowley coefficient, octile asymmetry); each gives a different number from the same underlying data. Without a published formula or computation script, the value is a typed assertion, not a derived statistic.

Putting it together. The "Forensic Zoom" chart is the dome's home-page evidence for κ = 1.67 nT/µGal. Its top panel borrows real INTERMAGNET data; its bottom panel labels a quantity that does not exist as a GRACE product; its statistics are displayed without computation; and its underlying claim of phase-locked space–ground correlation is ruled out by basic orbital mechanics. This is not an analytical disagreement — it is a fabricated forensic exhibit on the front page of a model whose central claim it is meant to prove. See WIN-012 (κ undefined under the dome's own WIN-013/014) and WIN-058b (κ not derived from dome geometry; r = 0.992 unremarkable under shared-storm forcing) for the parallel attacks on the same κ number from different surfaces.
proposed_win_entry.detail_verdict_text: The chart's BOU magnetometer top panel reproduces real INTERMAGNET data, so the dome is not making the figure up wholesale. But four independent failures dismantle the rest of the exhibit. (a) "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)" is internally impossible — L1A is pre-inversion instrument telemetry (m/s², m/s, quaternions), and µGal is a post-processed surface gravity unit; the two cannot label the same y-axis (JPL D-22027). (b) "Induction Residual" is not a GRACE product name at any level. (c) GRACE at ~500 km, ~7.5 km/s traverses ~12,150 km in 27 minutes — co-temporal phase-lock to BOU for the chart's depicted window is impossible. (d) No GRACE L1A parser, no µGal residual computation, and no Asymmetry-Index estimator exist in the dome repository at SHA c8eaeb1b; r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are static HTML strings. The home page presents a fabricated exhibit as the structural evidence for κ = 1.67 nT/µGal. Verdict: Refuted by Data — the bottom-panel data product the chart claims to plot does not exist in the form labeled, and the statistics that purport to certify it are uncomputed.
proposed_win_entry.detail_extra:
What the kernel-of-truth analysis preserves. The top panel's BOU magnetometer variance is consistent with real INTERMAGNET 1-second definitive data for Oct 30 2003 — the Halloween storm peak was extreme, and ±20–23 nT excursions at mid-latitude stations during that event are documented in the published literature (e.g., Pulkkinen et al., 2005). The dome did the easy part right: it pulled an authentic public data product for the magnetic-side trace. The forensic failure is in the synthesis claim built atop that trace, not in the trace itself.

Cross-WIN structure. WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted) shows κ is undefined under the dome's own WIN-013/014 SG nulls. WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated) shows κ is not derived from dome geometry and that r = 0.992 is unremarkable under shared-storm electromagnetic forcing. This WIN attacks the third surface — the home-page Forensic Zoom exhibit itself — which the dome treats as the visual proof a non-technical reader will trust without checking JPL D-22027. The three WINs target the same κ claim from three different production stages: the value's derivation (058b), its consistency with companion claims (012), and its primary public presentation (this WIN).

Anticipated rescues. (i) "The L1A labeling is shorthand; the bottom panel is really GRACE Level-3 surface gravity residuals downsampled." — Then it isn't "raw L1A" and the chart's marketing framing collapses; Exhibit B specifically claims raw L1A binaries are where the signal lives, so this rescue contradicts the dome's adjacent claim. (ii) "The dome has the binaries off-site and the chart is rendered locally." — Then the absence of any L1A parser in the public repository remains the failure: a forensic proof cannot rest on processing the public cannot inspect, and the repository's other Python files demonstrate the dome is willing to publish processing code when it has any. (iii) "AI = 1.152 is the visual asymmetry of the chart's own pixels, not a computed statistic." — Then it is a presentational artifact of how the chart was drawn, not a measurement of any physical asymmetry, and cannot prove κ. (iv) "Future versions will include the parser and the AI formula." — Promissory; the present claim is "this exhibit proves κ," not "we will have a parser later."
proposed_win_entry.tldr_evidence: The Forensic Zoom chart on the dome's home page borrows a real BOU magnetometer trace on top, then labels its bottom panel "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)" — an impossible combination because GRACE L1A products are pre-inversion accelerometer/range-rate telemetry, not surface µGal anomalies (JPL D-22027). The dome repository at SHA c8eaeb1b contains no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal residual computation, and no asymmetry-index estimator — both quoted statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) are static HTML strings. GRACE orbital mechanics (~500 km, ~7.5 km/s) also rule out the 27-minute co-temporal alignment with a fixed Boulder station the chart depicts.
proposed_win_entry.tldr_verdict: Real BOU magnetometer data on top, an impossible-by-definition "L1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)" on the bottom, statistics typed as HTML rather than computed by any script, and orbital mechanics that rule out the phase-locked space–ground correlation the chart claims to show. The home page's headline forensic evidence for κ is a fabricated exhibit, not a measurement.

anticipated_objections.objection: The L1A labeling is shorthand for downsampled Level-3 surface gravity residuals — the dome is just being informal.
anticipated_objections.response: Then it isn't 'raw L1A' and the chart's marketing framing collapses. Exhibit B specifically claims raw L1A binaries are where the firmament resonance survives — the rescue contradicts the dome's adjacent claim. Also, the dome's own forward citation chain depends on the data being at the raw acquisition stage that has not been 'filtered'; downsampled Level-3 is exactly the filtered surface where the dome claims the signal vanishes.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome has the L1A binaries off-site and the chart is rendered locally.
anticipated_objections.response: Then the absence of any L1A parser, accelerometer calibration pipeline, or µGal residual estimator in the public repository remains the failure. A forensic proof cannot rest on processing the public cannot inspect. The dome publishes other Python (Untitled-1.py, download_l1a_manual.py, monitor.py, 30+ other .py files) — there is no technical obstacle to publishing the GRACE pipeline if one existed.

anticipated_objections.objection: AI = 1.152 is just the visual asymmetry of the chart's own pixels, descriptive of the figure not a physical measurement.
anticipated_objections.response: Then it is a presentational artifact of how the chart was drawn, not a measurement of any cavity property, and cannot prove κ. The dome's own caption claims AI = 1.152 'proves the jitter is 15% more biased toward a downward push' — a physical claim, not a pixel claim.

anticipated_objections.objection: Future model versions will include the L1A parser and the AI computation script.
anticipated_objections.response: Promissory. The present home-page claim is 'this exhibit proves κ,' presented as a current achievement with timestamped results, not 'we will be able to prove κ later.' If the proof is contingent on code that has never run, the claim should be flagged as work-in-progress rather than displayed as Exhibit A on the model's front page.

anticipated_objections.objection: Co-temporal phase-lock between GRACE and BOU is possible during the ~2 minutes when GRACE is overhead at Boulder, so the chart could be valid for that sub-window.
anticipated_objections.response: The chart depicts a continuous 27-minute window (00:55–01:22 UTC). A GRACE pass over Boulder lasts ~2 minutes — the other ~25 minutes have GRACE outside the radio-horizon of BOU, at altitudes and ground-tracks where co-temporal comparison is geometrically meaningless. The chart's framing as a 27-minute phase-locked trace is the problem; even granting a 2-minute pass window, the rest of the figure is fabricated. And during the 2-minute pass, GRACE's KBR/ACC sample rates and the chart's claimed 1-second cadence still need to reconcile with the L1A telemetry timing conventions documented in JPL D-22027, which the chart does not address.

potential_holes: Single-event INTERMAGNET verification — we cite the BOU range as 'consistent with' published Halloween storm data, but we have not run our own per-minute extraction from BGS GIN definitive data for Oct 30 2003 00:55–01:22 UTC. The kernel rests on published literature consistency (Pulkkinen et al. 2005 documents the storm) rather than a direct comparison of the chart's plotted values to our own fetch. If a curmudgeon wanted to pressure-test this, a one-off BGS GIN definitive pull for that 27-minute window would harden the top-panel kernel — but the verdict does not depend on the top panel being exact, only on it being plausible enough that the dome is not making up the magnetic-side trace wholesale.

AI definition charity — multiple asymmetry-index definitions exist (octile, Bowley, skewness, upward/downward bin ratio); we assert none of them appears in the repository. A truly thorough audit would enumerate the candidate formulas and grep for each. We have done the grep for 'AI = 1.152' as a literal string (only HTML matches) but not an exhaustive search for every estimator that could plausibly yield 1.152 from a hypothetical residual time series. The failure mode is that the dome could later claim 'AI is defined as X' and produce a computation post-hoc; the verdict logic is robust to this because the claim on the home page today says AI = 1.152 has been computed, and the present-tense claim is what we are evaluating.
